records.ety: [F. gomtrie, L. geometria, fr. Gr. <?/, fr. <?/ to measure land; ge`a, gh^, the earth + <?/ to measure. So called because one of its earliest and most important applications was to the measurement of the earth's surface. See Geometer.]

defs: That branch of mathematics which investigates the relations, properties, and measurement of solids, surfaces, lines, and angles; the science which treats of the properties and relations of magnitudes; the science of the relations of space.
